title: [architect] Decide GitHub App, trigger, secrets, and runner metrics integration
objective: Resolve FlexNetOS runner/GitHub App integration for the supervisor: choose trigger mode (cron/polling, GitHub webhook through flexnetos_github_app, manual command, workflow_dispatch, or handoff loop), decide where repair agents run, define secret minting/scoping, and feed runner evaluator metrics into lane-capacity decisions.
acceptance_criteria: A committed architecture note chooses the supervisor trigger path for v1 and records rejected alternatives with reasons.
Secret minting/scoping is defined, including GitHub App permissions and TTLs for scan, rerun, update-branch, push, and merge operations.
Decision records whether workers run as local sessions, GitHub Actions jobs, or hybrid lanes, and how that affects runner capacity.
Runner evaluator outputs are mapped into supervisor capacity/health decisions instead of relying on ad-hoc runner-status assumptions.

source_gap_audit.reason: Expanded from .handoff/.idea intent after the initial TASK-PRR set proved too broad.
